questions.question: Compare I²C and SPI communication protocols in terms of wires required, speed, and number of devices supported.
keyPoints: I²C uses 2 wires, SPI uses 4 wires
SPI offers higher speed than I²C
I²C supports more devices on a single bus with addressing
SPI requires separate chip select for each device

questions.question: Explain how you would design an IoT weather monitoring system using Raspberry Pi, describing the interfaces you would use for different sensors and why.
keyPoints: Temperature sensor using I²C for multiple sensors on same bus
Rain sensor using analog input with ADC via SPI
Wind speed sensor using GPIO interrupts for counting

questions.question: Explain the concept of PWM (Pulse Width Modulation) and describe how it can be used in an IoT application on Raspberry Pi.
keyPoints: PWM simulates analog output by rapidly toggling a digital pin
Duty cycle controls the effective voltage (0-100%)
Common IoT uses include LED brightness control and motor speed control
Python implementation using GPIO.PWM(pin, frequency) and ChangeDutyCycle()

questions.question: Design an IoT system that uses at least three different Raspberry Pi interfaces. Describe the hardware setup, communication protocols used, and the complete data flow.
keyPoints: Example: Smart greenhouse using I2C for temperature/humidity sensor, SPI for soil moisture ADC, UART for GPS location tracking
Detailed data flow from sensor reading through processing to cloud upload
Justification for each protocol choice based on requirements
